# UC-18.3.15 · BGP EVPN Route Table Convergence

## Description

Measures time-to-stable route count after churn events (link bounce, leaf reboot) — complements route count trending (UC-18.3.10).

## Value

Measures time-to-stable route count after churn events (link bounce, leaf reboot) — complements route count trending (UC-18.3.10).

## Implementation

Simplify: alert on `total_routes` delta spikes; use scripted convergence test after maintenance. Tune thresholds to fabric size.

## Detailed Implementation

Prerequisites
• Install and configure the required add-on or app: BGP monitor, `evpn:route_summary`.
• Ensure the following data sources are available: `evpn:route_summary`.
• For app installation, inputs.conf, and Splunk directory layout, see the Implementation guide: docs/implementation-guide.md

Step 1 — Configure data collection
Simplify: alert on `total_routes` delta spikes; use scripted convergence test after maintenance. Tune thresholds to fabric size.

Step 2 — Create the search and alert
Run the following SPL in Search (then save as report or alert; adjust time range and threshold as needed):

```spl
index=network sourcetype="evpn:route_summary" earliest=-24h
| sort 0 host _time
| streamstats global=f last(total_routes) as prev_routes by host
| eval churn=abs(total_routes-prev_routes)
| where churn>500
| table _time, host, total_routes, prev_routes, churn
```

Understanding this SPL

**BGP EVPN Route Table Convergence** — Measures time-to-stable route count after churn events (link bounce, leaf reboot) — complements route count trending (UC-18.3.10).

Documented **Data sources**: `evpn:route_summary`. **App/TA** (typical add-on context): BGP monitor, `evpn:route_summary`. The SPL below should target the same indexes and sourcetypes you configured for that feed—rename `index=` / `sourcetype=` if your deployment differs.

The first pipeline stage scopes events using **index**: network; **sourcetype**: evpn:route_summary. That sourcetype matches what this use case lists under Data sources.

**Pipeline walkthrough**

• Scopes the data: index=network, sourcetype="evpn:route_summary", time bounds. Cross-check against **Data sources** above so indexes and sourcetypes match your ingestion.
• Orders rows with `sort` — combine with `head`/`tail` for top-N patterns.
• `streamstats` rolls up events into metrics; results are split **by host** so each row reflects one combination of those dimensions.
• `eval` defines or adjusts **churn** — often to normalize units, derive a ratio, or prepare for thresholds.
• Filters the current rows with `where churn>500` — typically the threshold or rule expression for this monitoring goal.
• Pipeline stage (see **BGP EVPN Route Table Convergence**): table _time, host, total_routes, prev_routes, churn

Enable Data Model Acceleration (and metric indexes for `mstats`) for the models or datasets referenced above; otherwise `tstats`/`mstats` may return no results from summaries.


Step 3 — Validate
Confirm results in the index, then compare with the same scope in the relevant fabric/SDN controller or manager UI.

Step 4 — Operationalize
Add the search to a dashboard or set up alert actions (email, webhook, PagerDuty, etc.) as required. Document the use case in your runbook and assign an owner. Consider visualizations: Line chart (total routes), Table (churn events), Bar chart (max churn by leaf).

Scripted input (generic example)
This use case relies on a scripted input. In the app's local/inputs.conf add a stanza such as:

```ini
[script://$SPLUNK_HOME/etc/apps/YourApp/bin/collect.sh]
interval = 300
sourcetype = your_sourcetype
index = main
disabled = 0
```

The script should print one event per line (e.g. key=value). Example minimal script (bash):

```bash
#!/usr/bin/env bash
# Output metrics or events, one per line
echo "metric=value timestamp=$(date +%s)"
```

For full details (paths, scheduling, permissions), see the Implementation guide: docs/implementation-guide.md

## SPL

```spl
index=network sourcetype="evpn:route_summary" earliest=-24h
| sort 0 host _time
| streamstats global=f last(total_routes) as prev_routes by host
| eval churn=abs(total_routes-prev_routes)
| where churn>500
| table _time, host, total_routes, prev_routes, churn
```

## Visualization

Line chart (total routes), Table (churn events), Bar chart (max churn by leaf).
